name: Kingshot
methodology: Open web HTML fetch from the preselected low-login-risk sources. For each source, extract event-related headings, paragraphs, links, and image URLs; follow a small number of event-like links for detail pages.

relevant_text_blocks: Kingshot - Official Wiki | Century Games © 2026
Kingshot - Official Wiki | Century Games © 2026
Kingshot - Official Wiki | Century Games © 2026

In this tab, you can see the total resources and its production per hour for your entire alliance. The resources allows your alliance to conduct research, build flags, and build HQ’s. In order to obtain more resources, your alliance flag(s) must incorporate the Alliance Resource Nodes into its range.
Territory Buildings:

The first building you will see in this tab is the “Badland HQ”. This is the first HQ that an alliance is capable of building at the start of the game. This building is the heart of your Alliance’s territory.

The last building you will see in this tab is the “Alliance Banner”. The banners are available for alliances to build and expand their territory, connecting to outposts and obtaining Alliance Resource Nodes.

Within this tab you will be able to find the Event Buildings and Prestige Building(s) won by your alliance. The first building you will see is the “Hunting Trap 1” which allows your alliance to start a “Bear Hunt” in order to obtain Forgehammer(s), Resources, Lucky Hero Gear Chest, Enhancement XP Part, and Alliance Tokens.
The second building in this tab, unlocked later on, is the Hunting Trap 2, which allows an alliance to set a bear hunt for another specific time for their members living across the globe.

The last section in the “Special Buildings” tab is the Prestige Building. These buildings are Honor Rewards received through alliance events, such as Alliance Championship, which gives gathering buffs to your alliance members.
Kingshot - Official Wiki | Century Games © 2026

Sanctuary & Fortress Sanctuaries and Fortresses are special buildings located across the world map. When controlled by an alliance, they provide valuable rewards to all alliance members. These rewards help players grow stronger and better prepare for upcoming events
Once the protection period ends, alliances can sign up and compete for control of these locations

During the battle phase, alliances fight to capture and hold these buildings. Multiple alliances may target the same Sanctuary or Fortress, which often leads to large battles as each alliance tries to secure control
Rewards for all alliance members
Stronger preparation for future events
Contribution to seasonal progress
Because of this, Sanctuaries and Fortresses are important objectives for alliances that want to stay competitive throughout the season
Alliance Leaders and R4 members are responsible for signing the alliance up for Sanctuary and Fortress battles
During each round, an alliance can manually sign up for 1 Sanctuary battle and 1 Fortress battle
When selecting a battle, the alliance must choose from available time slots
The final battle time will be determined based on the time period chosen by the majority of participating alliances
Participating in Battles
Once the battle phase begins, registered players can send troops to capture and garrison the target building
Players must already be members of the alliance before battle day to participate
Leaving and joining another alliance on the same day will prevent participation in the battle for the new alliance

⚠ Important: If your alliance won a Sanctuary or Fortress in the previous round , the alliance will be automatically signed up to defend it in the next round . This automatic sign-up does not count toward the normal sign-up limit , meaning the alliance can still manually register for 1 additional Sanctuary and 1 additional Fortress battle
During the battle phase, alliances fight to gain control of a Sanctuary or Fortress
To gain full control , an alliance must hold the building continuously for 30 minutes during the battle phase

event_detail_pages.meta_description: Outpost Description Outposts are building scattered across the map that provides valuable buffs to all alliances members of the alliance controling them. These buffs are helpful for all alliances members to help them grow and to improve their stats for events. To be able to control an Outpost, an alliance must connect its territory to
relevant_text_blocks: Outposts are building scattered across the map that provides valuable buffs to all alliances members of the alliance controling them.
These buffs are helpful for all alliances members to help them grow and to improve their stats for events.
To be able to control an Outpost, an alliance must connect its territory to it. This can be done by building alliance banners or HQ next to the Outpost .

Control the Outpost for the designated period to occupy it (Lv. 1: 15 minutes; Lv. 2: 20 minutes; Lv. 3: 25 minutes; Lv.4: 30 minutes). If a Outpost is not occupied within 24 hours, its control will be given to the Alliance that previously occupied it. When a Outpost is occupied for the first time, the occupying Alliance will obtain the First Control Reward.
There are no casualties in outpost battles of levels 1, 2, and 3, only injured or severely injured troops. Battles at Level 4 outposts, however, can result in the loss of troops.

Plan your alliance banner placement early so your territory can reach important Outposts before other alliances. When a kingdom is first established, alliances that capture an Outpost for the first time receive a First Occupation Reward . This reward is distributed to all alliance members
You can only compete for Outposts that border your alliance territory . To claim territory, you must first place your Alliance HQ and expand using Alliance Banners

Higher level Outposts can cause significant troop losses during battles. Always scout the Outpost first to check enemy presence before joining the fight, and make sure your Infirmary has enough capacity to handle injured troops and prevent unnecessary losses
Occupying different types of Outposts grants the corresponding effect. Effects from the same Outpost type and level do not stack
